description_long: This is a two-story pagoda structure with a prominent multi-tiered roof, each tier featuring blue tiles with red ridges and upturned eaves, characteristic of East Asian architecture. The building is elevated on red columns, with an open lower level and a partially enclosed upper level, both accessible by a central staircase. The roof is divided into three main sections, with the central section slightly higher and flanked by two symmetrical wings. The exterior walls are primarily brown with red trim, and the structure sits on a circular grassy base with a stone path and a small set of stairs leading to the entrance. The overall design is ornate yet functional, with railings and decorative elements enhancing its visual appeal. The pagoda is likely constructed from wood and tile, with open air spaces between the columns and within the upper story.
description: A two-story East Asian pagoda with blue-tiled, upturned roofs, red columns, and brown walls, set on a grassy base with a stone path and stairs. The structure features ornate railings and a central staircase.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, central staircase, and symmetrical blue-tiled roofs with upturned eaves.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof tiers, the red columns, and the layered structure of the pagoda.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back symmetry, open lower level, and the continuation of the blue-tiled roof design.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the multi-tiered roof, red support columns, and the stone path leading to the entrance.
